assertions.reason: HTTP 200 with 100 tickers; sampled ticker included coin_id, target_coin_id, base, target, market, last, converted_last.usd, converted_volume.usd, and is_stale.
HTTP 200 with 3 filtered tickers; unique returned coin_ids were only ['bitcoin'].
Unsupported order returned HTTP 400 with error=invalid_parameter and a message naming the invalid order value.
Unknown exchange tickers route returned HTTP 404 with error=not_found and a missing-exchange message.
HTTP 200 returned 1708 timestamp-volume pairs; sampled rows were numeric, non-negative, and strictly chronological.
Both invalid range variants returned HTTP 400 with the invalid_parameter error family.
HTTP 200 returned coins ordered by ascending market_cap_rank [1,2,3,6,7,8,8], and every item kept score == market_cap_rank.
Exchange detail returned HTTP 200 with 100 embedded tickers; sampled ticker exposed live-fidelity fields and market.identifier='binance'.
One-day volume chart returned HTTP 200 with 83 recent points, preserving granular short-window history.
Unknown exchange volume_chart and volume_chart/range both returned HTTP 404 with the standard not_found envelope.
show_max=0 returned HTTP 200 with exactly { coins: [], nfts: [], categories: [] }.

Runtime tickers expose fresh live-backed fields and volume_chart returned 1731 retained live-history points over 7 days, while canonical docs now classify tickers as live-backed and volume_chart as hybrid-from-live.

frictions.description: Using /bin/sh for combined evidence commands printed 'printf: Illegal option --' when the format string started with dashes, but the curl requests still completed and the HTTP evidence remained usable.
frictions.resolution: Ignored the shell warning because it did not affect the request/response capture.

summary: Tested 12 assigned assertions: 12 passed and 0 failed.
